channel.title: Frontion - Geopolitical News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: ⚔️ Iran and US exchange fire as hostilities escalate across Gulf
item.description: Iran retaliated against US strikes with attacks on Kuwait and Bahrain, while the US military announced it completed additional strikes on multiple Iranian targets. Bahrain activated missile warning sirens and Kuwait temporarily closed its airspace.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 💰 Dollar wobbles as Middle East strikes unsettle markets
item.description: The US dollar fluctuated on Thursday as fresh US military strikes in the Middle East undermined sentiment, while a surge in May consumer inflation to a three-year high kept investors uneasy about the Federal Reserve outlook.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇦🇲 Trump praises Armenia PM election win despite Russian pressure
item.description: US President Donald Trump congratulated Armenian Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan on his Civil Contract party decisive victory in weekend elections, noting the win came despite significant Russian pressure on the country.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine strikes Russian-occupied Mariupol port
item.description: Ukrainian forces struck the Russian-occupied port of Mariupol, Kyiv said on Wednesday, the latest in a series of drone attacks on logistics across a critical stretch of Moscow-held southern Ukraine connecting Russia to Crimea.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🛢️ Iran threatens to target any ship in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran military command announced it will target any vessel transiting the Strait of Hormuz after striking two vessels in the strategic waterway, marking a significant escalation in tensions with the United States.
item.dc:creator: France24

item.title: 💰📈 US consumer inflation vaults above 4% as Iran war drives energy prices
item.description: US consumer inflation rose at its fastest pace in three years in May, with energy costs surging amid the Middle East conflict, giving the Federal Reserve ammunition to keep interest rates unchanged into 2027.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇺🇦⛽ Russian-held Sevastopol suspends fuel distribution after Ukraine strikes
item.description: The governor of Russian-occupied Sevastopol in Crimea delayed fuel distribution plans after Ukrainian drone attacks disrupted supply routes, the latest in Kyivs campaign targeting Russian logistics.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇪🇺📊 EU fiscal watchdog criticises loosening budget rules for energy transition
item.description: The European Fiscal Board warned that allowing governments to spend defence fiscal flexibility on clean energy transition undermines EU fiscal discipline, amid debates over how to fund both priorities.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇮🇳📉 Indian shares set to open lower on Middle East escalation and US inflation
item.description: Indian equities are likely to open lower as fresh escalation between the US and Iran and a sharp jump in US inflation trigger risk-off sentiment across global markets.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: ⛪🕊️ Pope Leo XIV blesses Gaudis Sagrada Familia on centenary of architects death
item.description: Pope Leo XIV celebrated Mass at Barcelonas iconic Sagrada Familia basilica exactly 100 years after architect Antoni Gaudis death, blessing the towering architectural masterpiece.
item.dc:creator: CNN

item.title: 🇪🇺 Five EU countries call for united response to Trump drug price threats
item.description: Five EU member states are pushing for a coordinated European response to US pressure on pharmaceutical pricing, warning against bilateral deals that could undermine the bloc.
item.dc:creator: Politico

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK-EU summit at risk as youth visa talks hit fresh obstacle
item.description: A planned UK-EU summit faces delays as negotiations over youth mobility visas stall, with both sides struggling to find common ground on migration terms.
item.dc:creator: Politico

item.title: 🇮🇳 India calls in air force to secure exam papers after scandal fuels protests
item.description: The Indian Air Force has been tasked with transporting and securing examination papers after cheating scandals sparked nationwide protests over exam integrity.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US Strikes Multiple Targets in Iran as Trump Vows Further Escalation
item.description: The US military launched new strikes against multiple targets in Iran overnight, with President Donald Trump threatening additional attacks if no peace deal is reached. Iran announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 🇬🇧 Police Fire Water Cannons at Belfast Protesters as Unrest Flares After Stabbing
item.description: Northern Ireland saw renewed violence as police used water cannons against protesters in Belfast following a stabbing incident. A Sudanese man appeared in court charged with attempted murder.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump White House UFC Fight Requires $60M and 7 Federal Agencies
item.description: President Donald Trump's planned UFC fight on the White House South Lawn requires a monumental effort from more than seven federal agencies and an estimated $60 million in costs.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇯🇵⚡ Japan Restarts Reactor at World's Largest Nuclear Plant Amid Energy Push
item.description: A reactor at the world's largest nuclear power plant in Japan has resumed operations as the country pushes to maximize nuclear power generation to cope with growing electricity needs and climate goals.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇮🇩💰 Indonesia Sovereign Wealth Fund Tests Investor Appetite With Debut Dollar Bond
item.description: Indonesia's sovereign wealth fund Danantara kicked off its debut global dollar bond sale, testing investor appetite at a time of market volatility and global uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg

item.title: 🇨🇳 Chinese AI Police Tech Measures Vital Signs and "Core Motivation" at Beijing Expo
item.description: An international police equipment expo in Beijing showcased surveillance cameras that measure vital signs and even "core motivation" of suspects, reflecting China's advancing AI-powered security capabilities.
item.dc:creator: SCMP

item.title: 🇺🇸 House Lawmakers Back 5-7% Military Pay Raise for 2027, No Increase for DoD Civilians
item.description: The House Armed Services Committee approved a 5% to 7% pay raise for service members in 2027, while Defense Department civilian employees would see no pay increase under the spending bill.
item.dc:creator: Federal News Network

item.title: 🇮🇳 India's Urban Boom Turns Cities Into Heat Traps as Climate Change Intensifies
item.description: India's rapid urbanization is making cities significantly hotter, with the urban heat island effect combining with climate change to create dangerous living conditions for millions.
item.dc:creator: Nikkei Asia

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Announces Strait of Hormuz Closure After US Launches New Strikes
item.description: Iran's military declared the strategic Strait of Hormuz closed to all vessels and threatened to fire on any ships attempting transit, following fresh US military strikes on multiple Iranian targets.
item.dc:creator: Straits Times

item.title: 🇧🇭 Bahrain sounds missile alert sirens as Iran vows retaliation for US strikes
item.description: Bahrain activated its missile alert system as Iran announced it would retaliate after the US launched new airstrikes. The tiny Gulf kingdom hosts the US Fifth Fleet and is within range of Iranian missiles.
item.dc:creator: AP News

item.title: 🇮🇱 Netanyahu to seek re-election despite Trump doubts
item.description: Benjamin Netanyahu will run for re-election this year, his party announced, after US President Donald Trump said he was not sure if the Israeli prime minister would stand again.
item.dc:creator: Reuters

item.title: 💰 Iran war anxiety doubles container shipping rates from Asia to US
item.description: The cost of shipping a container from Asia to the United States has doubled since the Iran war began, driven by soaring fuel prices and importers rushing to secure goods before costs rise further.
item.dc:creator: Reuters
